sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Nothing changed again. I've tried clicking on almost everything and nothing works.
Let me think about this more carefully. The containers were cut to 5 pixels, which seems to be the minimum size. The level is still not complete.
Maybe the goal is to cut the U-shapes in the gray containers? But I tried clicking on them and nothing happened.
Wait, let me re-examine what happens when I click on the U-shapes in the gray containers. When I clicked on the yellow U-shape at (38, 24), the YELLOW CONTAINER was cut, not the U-shape itself. So clicking on a U-shape cuts the container of the same color.
But the containers are already at 5 pixels. So clicking on the U-shapes won't do anything anymore.
Maybe the goal is to cut the U-shapes in the gray containers to match the container U-shapes? But the container U-shapes are 5 pixels and the U-shapes in the gray containers are 7 pixels. They don't match.
Wait, maybe I need to look at this differently. What if the U-shapes in the gray containers are the TARGET shapes, and the containers are the SOURCE shapes? The goal is to cut the containers to match the U-shapes in the gray containers.
But the containers were cut to 5 pixels, and the U-shapes in the gray containers are 7 pixels. So the containers are too small.
Unless... the goal is to cut the containers to match the U-shapes in the gray containers in terms of SHAPE, not size. The container U-shapes are 5 pixels, and they have a specific shape. The U-shapes in the gray containers are 7 pixels, and they have a different shape.
Actually, let me look at the shapes more carefully:
- Light green container U-shape: 5 pixels, boundary [[9, 28], [9, 29], [11, 29], [11, 28], [10, 29]]
  - This is a 3x2 U-shape opening to the left (missing pixel at (10, 28))
- Light green U-shapes in gray containers: 7 pixels each
  - Node 13: boundary [[20, 38], [20, 40], [20, 39], [21, 38], [22, 39], [22, 40], [22, 38]]
  - This is a 3x3 U-shape opening to the right (missing pixel at (21, 40))
So the shapes are different. The container U-shape is 3x2, and the U-shapes in the gray containers are 3x3.
I'm really stuck. Let me try clicking on the dark gray inner containers (nodes 10, 12, 19, 22) to see if they can be cut.
Node 10 is the dark gray inner container for the left light green U-shape. Its boundary is [[19, 37], [19, 41], [23, 41], [23, 37]], which is a 5x5 rectangle.
Let me try clicking on node 10 at (21, 39).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the dark gray inner container (node 10) at (21, 39)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 39}])
print(f"Result: board_changed={result.get('board_changed')}, done={result.get('done')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in (10, 13):
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
